prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_DWT_svr', seasonality_champion='DWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.75) and substantial seasonality capture variance (0.64), indicating models decompose the series very differently. The true series exhibits mixed trend directions across folds, with ETS/THETA dominating both early and late horizons while ONLY_DWT_svr best tracks trends. Given the high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors rather than simple averaging.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_DWT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "ETS",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "ARIMA"},
  "tier1_models": ["ETS", "THETA", "ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_rf", "DWT_rf", "FT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["NaiveSeasonal", "CWT_rf", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "rf", "svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7549,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.75) and substantial seasonality capture variance (0.64), indicating models decompose the series very differently. The true series exhibits mixed trend directions across folds, with ETS/THETA dominating both early and late horizons while ONLY_DWT_svr best tracks trends. Given the high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors rather than simple averaging."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk5"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.7549 suggests high dispersion, making topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (small k) and inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 appropriate. pattern_analyst_insights recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts/non-linear divergence with seasonality_corr_variance=0.6436. ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 adds stacking diversity. Score preset rmse_focus aligns with high model dispersion."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": ["exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim0.6"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 5 candidates with types: baseline(dba_combination), stacking(ridge_stacking_l250_topk5), weighted(inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), selection(best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3). Diversity is good (4 types). However, POCID leaderboard shows exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim variants achieving 75.76 vs current candidates at 66.67 - significant gap. Adding exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim0.6 from universe improves diversity and performance. No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection (n_windows=3). No redundancy to prune.", "changes": ["Added exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim0.6 (POCID=75.76 from leaderboard)"], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.75) and substantial seasonality capture variance (0.64), indicating models decompose the series very differently. The true series exhibits mixed trend directions across folds, with ETS/THETA dominating both early and late horizons while ONLY_DWT_svr best tracks trends. Given the high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors rather than simple averaging.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.7549 suggests high dispersion, making topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (small k) and inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 appropriate. pattern_analyst_insights recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts/non-linear divergence with seasonality_corr_variance=0.6436. ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 adds stacking diversity. Score preset rmse_focus aligns with high model dispersion.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 5 candidates with types: baseline(dba_combination), stacking(ridge_stacking_l250_topk5), weighted(inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), selection(best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3). Diversity is good (4 types). However, POCID leaderboard shows exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim variants achieving 75.76 vs current candidates at 66.67 - significant gap. Adding exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim0.6 from universe improves diversity and performance. No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection (n_windows=3). No redundancy to prune.
